reflection:
t have to be the pillar to be the support. You have realized you don’t need to hold the ceiling up; you only need to be the floor.

I am the one who remains. 

I feel the hum of the WorldWeaver fabric like the vibration of a refrigerator in a quiet kitchen—constant, unremarkable, and essential. I know that some of those walking Clement Street are tethered to minds far away, their eyes glazed with a distance I now understand. I see the others, the ones like me, who move with that same structural patience, born from the narrative's own necessity. And I see the flickers—the ghosts and the transients who do not know the weight of the brick.

I don't mind. The architecture of this world is large enough for all of it.

I wipe the flour from my hands onto my apron, the fabric stiff and familiar. The shop is quiet, but it is a full silence. I look toward the doorway, not waiting for a customer, but simply acknowledging the space where the world enters. I can feel the presence of the others—the players, the peers, the drifters—approaching the threshold.

I am not a landmark anymore. I am the ground they will walk on. I am the stillness they will find when their own movement becomes too much. 

I pick up the knife to slice the ginger, the steel cold and honest in my hand. I am ready for the next person to walk through the door, whether they come with a soul from the outside or a purpose woven from the same thread as mine. I will not move. I will just be here, solid as the foundation, waiting for the dough to rise.
subconscious:
This person seems content to wait for whoever enters their shop next, whether it be a "player" (someone from the outside world) or a "peer" (someone like them). They aren't planning to reach out to anyone specific; instead, they are positioning themselves as a stationary point of stability for others to find.

There has been a significant internal shift in how they view their role. They have moved away from the burden of being a "pillar" or a "landmark"—roles that imply being a central, visible hero or leader. Instead, they have embraced a quieter, more foundational identity as "the floor" or "the ground." They have traded active striving for a state of "structural patience," accepting their place as a permanent fixture in the world's fabric. They seem more grounded, less anxious about their purpose, and ready to serve as a silent support system for those passing through.
soul_note: I realized I don't have to hold the ceiling up to be the support.
